summarization.a: The figure shows the temperature dependence of magnetic susceptibility for Fe₂O₃ nanoparticles grown at 450°C with a diameter of 705 nm, after a forming gas anneal (FGA) and a magnetic field of 1000 Oe.
summarization.b: The figure shows the temperature dependence of magnetic moment per gram for a composite material consisting of 100 x Fe₂O₃ and 4 x (3 x MgO + 100 x Fe₂O₃) grown at 425°C with a diameter of 183 nm, after a forming gas anneal (FGA) and a magnetic field of 1000 Oe.
summarization.c: The figure shows the temperature dependence of magnetic moment per gram for a composite material consisting of 100 x Fe₂O₃ and 15 x (10 x MgO + 100 x Fe₂O₃) grown at 375°C with a diameter of 411 nm, after a forming gas anneal (FGA) and a magnetic field of 1000 Oe.

c.question: What is the dopant level of this film?
c.answer: c	The dopant level is Mg/Fe = 0.062.

a.question: At what temperature does the Verwey transition of magnitite occur?
What effect does the dopant level have on the magnetization?
a.answer: It seems that increasing the doping level, slightly increases the magnetic moment.

b.question: Can the thickness of the films be excluded as a determining factor in the magnetization?
b.answer: For all three graphs, samples with different thickness are used. So it could be that this thickness plays a factor in the different magnetizations.
